description: Queries are embedded with a different model than the index, collapsing recall with no error raised.
body: Failure mode: an index built with one embedding model is queried using a different model or version (or different pooling/normalization), so query and document vectors occupy incomparable spaces and relevant results stop surfacing. Detection signals: a sharp recall drop with no exception, and a config or deploy diff showing the query-time embedder differs from the index-time embedder version. Do not retune ANN parameters to chase the lost recall. Safer fallback: pin the query and index embedding model to the same id and version, re-embed the corpus if the index model is unknown, and verify with a smoke query that a known item returns its known match.
